title: nigella arvensis var. hispanica
intro: Nigella arvensis var. hispanica, also known as Spanish fennel or Spanish black cumin, is a type of flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and relieving digestive issues, and it has been used to treat conditions like arthritis, bronchitis, and menstrual cramps. Nigella arvensis var. hispanica can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or oil, and it is also used as a spice in some traditional recipes.
